name: omc
description: Create, edit, debug, rebuild, or codesign OMC applets for macOS. Use when the user asks to build/rebuild or codesign an applet, add a command, write an action script, configure Command.plist or Command.json, use omc_dialog_control, chain commands, or work with AppletBuilder (incl. the appletbuilder CLI / Build & Run). For ActionUI JSON UI design, also activate the actionui skill.

claude.label: Claude (Anthropic Claude Code skill)
claude.notes: Delivered via Claude Code SKILL.md protocol. Full prose, tables OK. Script execution available. Can read docs/ for detail. Levels 1+2.

capable.label: Capable frontier models (Gemini, Grok, GPT-4o, Llama-3-70B+)
capable.notes: Delivered as system prompt. Standard Markdown + tables. No shell exec assumed. Levels 1+2. Targets Gemini 2.x, Grok 3+, GPT-4o, strong open-source models. Reassess annually.

lite.label: Small / local models (Phi-3, Mistral-7B, Llama-3-8B class)
lite.notes: Delivered as system prompt. Level 1 only. No macOS dev knowledge assumed. Inline examples. Keyword triggers. Tables stripped.

content.description: What OMC is + applet bundle structure + Command.plist format + script naming
Key environment variables + six runtime tools with usage snippets
Hard rules for AI agents: sh-not-bash, init placement, table/picker runtime semantics, debug-log workflow
All execution modes, activation modes, dialog integration keys, app lifetime events
NIB + ActionUI dialog integration overview; cross-reference to ActionUI skill
AppletBuilder workflow: create, edit commands/scripts/UI, build
Shared lib pattern, state management, table feeding, command chaining
Inline few-shot examples for lite/local models
How to run the Command.plist verifier and act on its findings
